account_signals.author: unknown
account_signals.text:
I think ranges/horizontal levels are the most important factor when looking at pairs.

This is because even as value of an asset changes, relative value between two assets should stay the same so long as nothing unique to one of those assets changes. In other words, asset pairs should always trade in a range until something changes that is specific to one or both parts of the pair unequally.

Let's do an example with cars and houses. If an entry level car is $30k, then likely the current entry l

search_signals.text:
The White House just sent Kevin Warsh's official nomination for Fed Chair (and Board member) to the Senate today.

The prediction market (on Kalshi) only resolves "yes" once the Senate formally receives it—not just when it's sent/announced.

So there's still uncertainty and trading room left before it settles.

search_signals.author: unknown
search_signals.text:
Bettors wagered $54 million on Khamenei’s death. Now they’re not getting paid.
Traders on the prediction-market site Kalshi assumed they had won a fortune by correctly betting on the Iranian leader’s demise. The ensuing scandal has thrown a spotlight on the “dystopian” rise of gambling on real-world events. By Drew Harwell https://t.co/mtImza8wDu

search_signals.author: unknown
search_signals.text:
count the platforms operating in prediction markets right now you can probably get to around 18-20 credible and generally well-funded platforms

maybe 5-6 survive to 2030

the ones that make it will have one of three things:

dominant liquidity in a specific category 

a CFTC-regulated moat expensive enough to deter new entrants 

vertical depth that makes generalist platforms irrelevant in their lane (some interesting platforms coming up that fit this)

everything else is burning runway waiting

the best thing you can do post-launch is keep your chart alive. consistent buys, active trading, and real volume signal to dexscreener and https://t.co/88FLfJuWnU that people care. a dead chart = nobody scrolling past it.

community helps too but the chart is what catches new eyes first.
